title: porophyllum ruderale var. macrolepidium
intro: Porophyllum ruderale var. macrolepidium, also known as Mexican tea or cilantro, is an annual herb native to the Americas, belonging to the sunflower family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat fever, coughs, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Porophyllum ruderale var. macrolepidium include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and aiding in digestion, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be made into a tea by infusing them in hot water, or used in tinctures and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
